registry_meta.purpose: Structured inventory of reusable feature bricks extracted from existing 864zeros extensions, organized by I/O contract and cross-referenced with strike ROI.

complexity_legend.XS: <= 50 LOC, single-purpose, no external deps beyond Chrome APIs
complexity_legend.S: 50-200 LOC, single concern, may have one external dep
complexity_legend.M: 200-500 LOC, multi-concern or with non-trivial external integration
complexity_legend.L: 500+ LOC, would not be classified as a single brick (out of scope)

registry_meta.v1_1_changelog: Added 3 build-kit bricks harvested from Strike 011 (BRK-DL-001, BRK-UI-002, BRK-UI-003); added ui-pattern category; added authority_chain pointing at BUILD_KIT_RULES.md and BACKLOG.md.
registry_meta.v1_2_changelog: Added BRK-UI-004 (accordion-record-v1) harvested from Strike 012 ScriptureScout. First directory-format brick. Status: PROMOTED. Pillar compatibility: Global (OIA, Flux, FHG). Authored under RULE-000 sign-off by Office Architect 2026-05-08.

changelog.change: BRK-UI-003 (two-tap-arm-pattern) authority_rule promoted from "RULE-001 §3" to "RULE-005". Three new rules codified in BUILD_KIT_RULES.md: RULE-005 (Two-Tap Destructive Confirmation, brick-backed), RULE-006 (Brand-Prefix Pill on Surface Titles, no brick), RULE-007 (Secret Sovereignty / BYOK, no brick). Atomic READMEs written for all 6 cross-repo Divisions (DIV-0, DIV-1, DIV-3, DIV-4, DIV-5, DIV-6).

contract.input: DOM document context (active tab via content script)
contract.output: { url, title, lang, description, keywords, links[], text, html, host, canonicalUrl, slug, siteName, section, author, publisher, datePublished, dateModified, contentType }

bricks.notes: Two variants exist. webinsights returns rich identifier metadata; clipboard returns simpler {text, url, title}. Recommend canonicalizing on the richer webinsights pattern.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Required to extract Web Highlights' annotation data via DOM inspection if no public API.

contract.output: { lang, metaDescription, og: {title, description, image, siteName, type}, twitter: {title, description, image} }

bricks.notes: Pure function. Companion mergeMetadataIntoItem() helper merges into a content item.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Useful for enriching migrated highlights with source-page metadata (og:title, og:image).

contract.output: { imageDataUrl: base64 png, bounds: {x, y, width, height} }

bricks.notes: Spans content script + background. Spec from CLAUDE.md: 'Marquee Selection (Critical): Reuse INITIATE_AREA_CAPTURE and CAPTURE_AREA_FROM_CONTENT logic'. Genuine differentiator vs other clippers.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Not needed for highlight migration; relevant for ClipGenius/visual-capture lanes.

contract.output: { pdfDataUrl: base64, filename, sizeBytes }

bricks.notes: Uses Chrome DevTools Protocol via debugger API. Four ready-to-use presets. Privacy-friendly (local generation, no cloud round-trip).
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Migration target is Markdown into vaults, not PDF. Skip for Strike 011.

source_paths: referenced in webinsights/background.js patterns; CLAUDE.md spec

contract.output: { imageDataUrl: base64 }

bricks.notes: Trivial wrapper around captureVisibleTab. Worth registering for canonical-shape consistency.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: n/a

contract.output: { redactedText: string, redactions: [{type, original, replacement, index}] }

bricks.notes: Marked BRK-PRIVACY-001. Patterns: EMAIL, PHONE, SSN, CC, IP, ADDRESS. Reversible via restore(text, redactions). Pure function. High-trust building block for any LLM-call privacy boundary.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Optional — useful if migrating highlights might leak PII into a synced vault. Recommend enabling by default in migration tools handling user-generated text.

contract.output: { summary: string, model: string }

bricks.notes: Worker URL is per-extension; for reuse, factor out the worker base URL into pricing.js or constants. Keys never leave the worker.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Migration tools don't need summarization. Useful for downstream products.

contract.output: { tags: string[], model: string }

bricks.notes: Same proxy pattern as summarize. Stateless.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: n/a

contract.output: { description: string, model: string }

dependencies: AI proxy worker (Gemini Vision under the hood)
bricks.notes: Pairs naturally with agent-marquee-capture. Together they form the 'Capture-and-Analyze' flow described in CLAUDE.md ClipGenius spec.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: n/a

contract.output: { response: string, model: string }

bricks.notes: Multi-turn conversation primitive. Useful for Q&A-over-clips features.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: n/a

contract.output: { suggestedTags: string[], confidence: number }

dependencies: pure JS, no API calls (keyword/stopword/frequency analysis)
bricks.notes: Free, offline, no AI bill. Inferior recall vs LLM, but zero-cost and zero-privacy-leak. Capabilities: tag_suggestions, similarity_matching, keyword_extraction. Good fallback / free-tier feature.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Optional — could auto-tag migrated highlights during the export step. Free-tier-friendly.

contract.input: { structured_diagram_json (matches DRAWIO_EXTRACTION_SCHEMA — diagram_type, title, layout_direction, grid_dimensions, legend, containers, nodes, edges) }
contract.output: { drawioXml: string }

dependencies: pure JS XML construction; pairs with Gemini Structured Output (DRAWIO_EXTRACTION_SCHEMA)
bricks.notes: Sophisticated. Includes a Gemini Structured Output schema for diagram extraction from images. Together with agent-ai-vision and agent-marquee-capture, forms the 'image-to-diagram' pipeline. Genuinely differentiated IP.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: n/a — different output format. But worth noting as an Option C lane in its own right (image-to-drawio is a niche export utility).

contract.input: { data: any, filename: string, appSlug: string }
contract.output: { success: bool, fileId: string, syncedAt: ISO8601 }
contract.additional_methods: init(clientId), connect() -> {success, email}, listBackups(), restore(fileId), delete(fileId)

bricks.notes: Marked '864zeros Feature Brick - Reusable across all extensions' in source. Uses drive.appdata scope (hidden app folder, no user-visible files). Auto-rotation up to MAX_BACKUPS = 5. Per-app namespacing via appSlug.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: VITAL — one of two viable migration destinations (Drive vault folder). Pairs with agent-markdown-converter (MISSING — see below) for the Web Highlights → Drive→Obsidian/Capacities flow.

contract.output: exportLocal: triggers .json download with shape {app, version, exportedAt, stores: {...}} / importLocal: {success, recordsImported, errors}

bricks.notes: Pure local: no cloud round-trip. Free tier. Schema-versioned (app+version fields). Pairs naturally with agent-drive-sync for Pro tier.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Pattern for the JSON intermediate: scrape Web Highlights → produce a versioned JSON dump → user downloads OR pipes to vault. Use this brick's shape verbatim.

contract.input: Standard CRUD: get(store, key), put(store, value), delete(store, key), getAll(store), exportAll(), importAll(data)
contract.output: Promise of value | array | success status

bricks.notes: Wrapper with schema-versioning support and exportAll/importAll for backup interop. The data spine of every panel-extension product the office has shipped.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Storage for staged migration data before final export. Standard pattern.

contract.output: Promise of value or success

bricks.notes: Per-extension settings + reactive UI updates. Single source of truth for settings (per CLAUDE-extension.md guidance).
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Useful for migration progress state (where the user left off in a multi-step migration).

contract.output: { allowed: bool, requiredTier: string|null, currentTier: string }

bricks.notes: Standard 864z tier check. Pairs with agent-payment-extpay. Skip entirely in --free build mode.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Not needed if MigrationPilot is OSS/free per recommendation in OR-2026-05-07-MIGRATION-PILOT.md.

contract.output: { user: ExtPayUser, paid: bool, plan: object, expiresAt: ISO8601 }

bricks.notes: Wraps ExtPay (which brokers Stripe under the hood). 5% ExtPay fee + ~2.9% Stripe fee = ~8% total transaction cost.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: Not needed — MigrationPilot recommended OSS.

contract.output: { ok: bool, remaining: int, history: [{type, feature, amount, timestamp}] }
contract.additional_methods: getBalance(), addCredits(amount, source), deduct(feature), canAfford(feature), getHistory(limit)

bricks.notes: Pay-per-use system for AI features. Initial free credits configurable. Append-only history (last 100 entries). Independent of tier-gating; can coexist.
bricks.migration_pilot_role: n/a

contract.input: Single: {content, title, source_url, timestamp, tags, note, extra?} | Batch: array of items + options
contract.process: 1) HTML/plaintext -> clean Markdown. 2) Inject YAML frontmatter (864z-metadata spec v1.0). 3) Sanitize filename for Windows/macOS/Linux.
contract.output: Single: {filename, markdown}. Batch: {files: [{filename, markdown}, ...]} (one-file-per-item with collision-protection, OR combined into single file when options.combined=true)

extracted_from: NEW — built fresh for Strike 011 MigrationPilot
bricks.notes: Pure ES2020. Self-test harness embedded (run with: node agent-markdown-converter.js — 5 tests, all pass). Public API: convertItem(), convertBatch(), htmlToMarkdown(), buildFrontmatter(), sanitizeFilename(). Defines the 864z-metadata spec v1.0 (frontmatter shape).
bricks.migration_pilot_role: VITAL — was the gating brick blocking Strike 011. Now built. Pairs with agent-drive-sync and agent-local-backup to complete the Web Highlights -> Markdown -> Obsidian/Capacities flow. Also unlocks 'Export to Obsidian' as a feature for clipboard, Bible-Insight, Chronicle, OIA-series.

contract.input: { content: string, filename: string, mimeType?: string, conflictAction?: 'uniquify'|'overwrite'|'prompt', saveAs?: boolean }
contract.output: Promise<number> — Chrome downloadId; rejects on download failure
contract.convenience_apis: downloadAsMarkdown(content, filename, opts), downloadAsJson(data, filename, opts), encodeUtf8Base64(s)

bricks.notes: Solves MV3 service worker URL.createObjectURL unreliability via Base64 data URI encoding chain (btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent(content)))). Pure ESM. No DOM access. Per RULE-002: URL.createObjectURL is FORBIDDEN inside background/service-worker.js — this brick is the canonical replacement. Public API: downloadAsDataURI, downloadAsMarkdown, downloadAsJson, encodeUtf8Base64.
bricks.applicable_strikes: any extension with SW-context downloads (export flows, Recovery PDF, JSON backups)
bricks.compliance_check: grep -r 'URL.createObjectURL' background/ → must return no matches outside comments

contract.input: Constructor: { masterCheckbox: HTMLInputElement, itemCheckboxSelector: string, getItemId: (cb)=>any, selectedClassTarget?, selectedClass?, masterLabelEl?, masterLabelTemplate?, onChange? }
contract.output: TristateSelection instance — { refresh(), getSelected(), getCount(), clear(), destroy() }
contract.behavior: Master checkbox tristate: all → checked, some → indeterminate, none → unchecked. Auto-syncs on DOM re-render via refresh(). Drops selections whose checkboxes no longer exist.

dependencies: OIA design system (oia-checkbox class for sage-green checked styling)
bricks.notes: Per RULE-003: any extension with a record/note/clip queue MUST use this pattern (or equivalent). Selective-action button must be disabled when 0 selected; action handler must refuse empty-selection requests server-side, not just rely on disabled UI.
bricks.applicable_strikes: any extension with a record queue + selective-action surface (clipboard, TabVault, Bible-Insight, ScriptureScout)
bricks.compliance_check: Action functions operate on getSelectedRecords(), not getAllRecords(); master checkbox + per-item checkboxes present in queue UI

contract.input: { button: HTMLButtonElement, onConfirm: ()=>any, hintEl?, defaultLabel?, armedLabel?, defaultHint?, armedHint?, armClass?, timeoutMs?, disableDuringConfirm? }
contract.output: controller — { cancelArm(), isArmed(), destroy() }
contract.behavior: First tap arms (visible state change via dataset.armed + class); second tap within timeoutMs confirms; outside-click or timeout cancels.

dependencies: DOM APIs only — no Chrome APIs, no design-system dependency
bricks.notes: Compliant with CLAUDE-base.md copy rules: no alert(), no confirm(), no 'Are you sure?' spam. The canonical destructive-confirmation primitive. Per RULE-001 §3: every Options page Clear-DB / Reset / Export-all action must use this pattern. Promoted to first-class RULE-005 (Two-Tap Destructive Confirmation) on 2026-05-08 — was previously cited only as a sub-clause of RULE-001 §3.
bricks.applicable_strikes: any extension with destructive controls (Clear DB, Reset to defaults, Wipe local cache)
bricks.compliance_check: grep -r 'window.alert\|window.confirm\|window.prompt' → must return no matches; destructive buttons in options/ wired through armDestructiveButton()

bricks.name: Interactive Record Accordion (v1)

contract.input: Constructor: { container: HTMLElement, recordSelector?, headerSelector?, chevronSelector?, bodyWrapperSelector?, expandedClass?, exclusive?: boolean (default true), shiftMultiExpand?: boolean (default true), onChange?: (Set) => void }
contract.output: AccordionController instance: { toggle(id, shiftKey?), expand(id), collapse(id), collapseAll(), isExpanded(id), getExpandedIds(), refresh(), destroy() }
contract.behavior: Header click toggles record; chevron rotates 90° on expand; body uses grid-template-rows transition for smooth height-auto; default exclusive expand (one open at a time); Shift+Click → multi-expand compare mode (REQUIRED per RULE-004).
contract.companion_helper: createChevronSVG() — canonical Bronze chevron markup (right-pointing arrow that rotates)

bricks.notes: FIRST DIRECTORY-FORMAT brick in the build-kit (ships JS + CSS + docs as a cohesive unit). Versioned suffix (-v1) anticipates v2 living alongside without breaking imports. Pillar-agnostic via --oia-* tokens — works in OIA Sage / 864-Flux Graphite / FHG Bronze without modification. Includes the Parchment Reading Surface standard (light reading panel even in dark themes). Promoted under RULE-000 sign-off (Office Architect 864z-OA) on 2026-05-08.
bricks.applicable_strikes: any extension with a queue-of-records UI (captures, notes, logs, todos, drafts, downloads, history)
bricks.compliance_check: Each record element has data-record-id; AccordionController instantiated with shiftMultiExpand: true (default OK); grid-template-rows transition present; chevron has rotation rule for .accordion-record--expanded; Shift+Click opens multiple records simultaneously

missing_bricks.status: NOT YET BUILT — possible future need
missing_bricks.rationale: Modern browsers expose File System Access API for direct writes to user-selected folders (e.g., an Obsidian vault on disk). This would let MigrationPilot write Markdown files directly to a user's vault folder without a Drive round-trip. Drive sync covers one path; direct FS writes cover the other.
missing_bricks.build_blocker_severity: OPTIONAL for MigrationPilot — Drive sync is sufficient if direct FS proves complex.
missing_bricks.recommended_action: Defer to Strike 011 build. If Drive flow ships first and works, add FS as v2.

useful_bricks: agent-anonymizer-pii (optional — privacy compliance for synced vaults)
skip_bricks: agent-ai-* (cloud AI not needed for raw migration)
migration_liberation_thesis.build_estimate: 5/5 essential bricks ready. agent-markdown-converter shipped (290 LOC, 0 deps, 5/5 self-test pass). Estimated ~6-10h remaining to integrate bricks into MigrationPilot v1 scaffold.

clipboard_gtm_readiness.linked_target: Move Clipboard from internal-build to Chrome Web Store launch
missing_for_clipboard_gtm: Marketing assets (icons, screenshots, store listing copy) — DIV-4 STUDIO scope
Privacy policy + terms-of-service URLs (mentioned in pricing.js but need real URLs)
Real ExtPay account ID (currently placeholder per BRK-PRICING-001 patterns)
clipboard_gtm_readiness.synergy_with_migration_pilot: agent-markdown-converter is now built and registered. Clipboard can integrate it directly to gain an 'Export to Obsidian / Capacities' feature with no additional engineering — opens cross-promotion narrative ('rescue your clips into your vault').

essential: custom Instapaper CSV parser (NOT in this registry — strike-specific)
864z-2026-003_INSTARESCUE.notes: Strike-specific custom parser is the bulk of work; bricks are foundational support.

864z-2026-004_PASSVAULT.notes: Highly custom build — security primitives dominate. Bricks are minimal here.

essential: custom EPUB builder (factory has it under lib/)
864z-2026-005_READFLOW.notes: Phase 1-6 complete per OR-2026-03-18-READFLOW. Outstanding work is polish/proof, not new bricks.

864z-2026-006_ROAMRESCUE.notes: Same pattern as 003: custom parser is the core differentiator.

roi_mapping.strategic_observation: Across 4 historical strikes (003, 004, 005, 006), the per-strike custom code is dominated by FORMAT-SPECIFIC PARSERS (Instapaper CSV, Dashlane CSV, Roam JSON). The bricks in this registry are foundational support — necessary but not the differentiator. For Strike 011 MigrationPilot, the Web Highlights data extraction will be similar: a custom scraper + the existing capture/storage/export bricks. The single missing brick (agent-markdown-converter) is the only non-strike-specific work needed to unblock the migration thesis lane.

audit_summary.v1_1_count_corrections: v1.0 audit_summary had off-by-one errors (claimed 21 cataloged with actual 20; infrastructure 6 vs actual 5; both_clipboard_and_webinsights 4 vs actual 3). v1.1 recounts directly from the bricks array.
ready_to_assemble_now: MigrationPilot (Strike 011) — 5/5 essential bricks ready; agent-markdown-converter shipped 2026-05-07; build-kit bricks BRK-DL-001/UI-002/UI-003 harvested 2026-05-08
Clipboard GTM — 100% bricks ready; can also adopt build-kit BRK-DL-001 (RULE-002), BRK-UI-002 (RULE-003), BRK-UI-003 (RULE-001) for compliance
ScriptureScout (Strike 012, charter draft) — Faith/Heritage liberation lane; reuses MigrationPilot stack + new build-kit bricks; needs custom Bible-source parsers (verse refs, citation frontmatter)
audit_summary.compliance_audit_pending: All extensions other than migration-pilot require RULE-001 / RULE-002 / RULE-003 audits per BACKLOG.md migration tracker.
